title: Spaghetti Aglio e Olio
notes: The simplest pasta dish. Quality of olive oil matters here.

stages.label: Pasta
stages.method: Bring a large pan of water to a rolling boil. Add salt and cook spaghetti until al dente per packet instructions. Reserve 100ml pasta water before draining.

stages.label: Oil and garlic
stages.method: While pasta cooks, warm oil in a wide pan over low heat. Add garlic and chilli and cook gently until garlic is golden but not brown, about 3 minutes. Add drained pasta, a splash of pasta water, and parsley. Toss vigorously to emulsify. Serve immediately.

title: Chicken Tikka Masala
notes: Marinating overnight gives the best result. The tikka (charring) step is optional but adds depth.

stages.label: Marinade
stages.method: Combine all marinade ingredients in a large bowl. Add chicken and coat thoroughly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, overnight if possible.

stages.label: Base aromatics
stages.method: Heat oil in a large heavy-based pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and cardamom; fry 30 seconds until fragrant. Add onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until deep golden — about 12 minutes. Add garlic and ginger paste and cook a further 2 minutes.

stages.label: Sauce
stages.method: Add ground spices to the onion base and fry 1 minute. Add tomatoes and sugar. Simmer uncovered for 15 minutes until sauce thickens and oil separates at the edges. Blend smooth if preferred. Stir in cream and simmer a further 5 minutes. Season.

stages.label: Cook and combine chicken
stages.method: Remove chicken from marinade. Either grill under a high broiler for 8–10 minutes, turning once, until lightly charred; or fry in batches in a hot oiled pan. Add cooked chicken to the sauce and simmer together for 5 minutes. Finish with fresh coriander.

title: Focaccia
notes: Makes one large tray. Scale for the tin size — this is written for a 30x40cm roasting tin.

stages.label: Dough
stages.timing: overnight cold prove, or 2 hours at room temperature
stages.method: Combine flour, yeast and salt in a large bowl. Add water and 2 tbsp of the oil. Mix to a rough dough then knead for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Oil the bowl, return dough, cover and refrigerate overnight or leave at room temperature for 2 hours until doubled.

stages.label: Tray and toppings
stages.method: Pour 2 tbsp oil into the roasting tin and spread. Tip in the dough and stretch gently to fill the tin. Drizzle remaining oil over the top. Leave to prove 30 minutes until puffed. Dimple aggressively with fingers. Press tomatoes and rosemary into the dimples. Scatter flaky salt.

stages.label: Bake
stages.method: Preheat oven to 220°C fan. Bake for 20–25 minutes until deep golden on top and pulling away from the edges. Cool in the tin for 10 minutes before cutting.

stages.label: Mix & knead

ingredients.item: 500g/1lb 2oz strong white flour, plus extra for kneading
40ml / 36g /1½fl oz olive oil, plus extra for kneading
extra oil and flour, for kneading

stages.method: Place the dry ingredients in a bowl (salt and yeast not touching). Add the oil and 240ml of water. Manually mix together, gradually adding remaining water until incorporated into a soft rough dough. Knead until smooth and silky. Place into a clean oiled bowl, cover and leave in a warm place until tripled in size.

stages.label: Shape & first prove
stages.method: Knock the dough back by folding it in on itself repeatedly. Flatten into a rectangle, fold each end into the middle, then gently roll into a Swiss roll shape with a smooth top and seam along the base. Place onto parchment/tin, cover and leave to prove for 1–2 hours until doubled in size.

stages.label: Bake
stages.method: At end of hour 1, preheat oven to 220°C/425°F/Gas 7 and place a baking tray of water on the bottom shelf. Lightly spray loaf with water and dust with flour. Make four diagonal slashes with a sharp knife. Bake for 25 minutes then lower heat to 200°C and bake a further 10 minutes. Cool on a wire rack.

stages.label: Dough & first prove

ingredients.item: 570 g all-purpose flour (you can also use 1/2 cup cake flour combined with 3 1/2 cups bread flour)
1 tablespoon active dry yeast (or instant yeast)
1 egg for Egg wash (beat 1 egg with 1 teaspoon water)
Simple syrup (1 tablespoon sugar, dissolved in 1 tablespoon hot water)

stages.method: In stand mixer with dough hook: add heavy cream, milk, egg, sugar, flour, yeast, and salt in that order. Mix on 'stir' for 15 minutes, adding flour 1 tbsp at a time if dough sticks to sides (it should stick to the bottom but not the sides). Cover with a damp towel and prove in a warm spot until doubled, 1–2 hours. (Tip: proof in a closed microwave with a mug of just-boiled water.)

stages.label: Shape & second prove
stages.method: Grease two baking vessels with butter. Return proved dough to mixer and stir 5 minutes to knock back air. Tip onto lightly floured surface, cut in half and shape. For loaves: form a rectangle, cut cross-wise into 3 pieces and place in loaf pan. For rolls: cut each half into 8 and roll into balls for a round pan. Cover and prove 1 hour. Preheat oven to 175°C.

stages.label: Bake & glaze
stages.method: Brush risen dough with egg wash. Bake for 23–25 minutes until golden brown. Remove from oven and immediately brush with sugar water (1 tbsp sugar dissolved in 1 tbsp hot water) to give shine and sweetness.

title: Braised Red Chinese Pork Belly (红烧肉)

stages.label: Blanch

ingredients.item: 500g skin-on pork belly, sliced into 3cm chunks
1 large knob of ginger, thinly sliced

stages.method: Add a few knobs of ginger and spring onion pieces to a pot of boiling water. Add a pinch of salt and the pork slices. Cook on high heat for 2–3 mins, then remove with a slotted spoon. Set aside. Discard the blanching liquid.

stages.label: Render fat
stages.method: Heat a large saucepan on medium heat and add the blanched pork. Cook for 10–15 mins until most of the fat has rendered. Remove pork with a slotted spoon. Pour the pork fat into a small bowl and set aside.

stages.label: Caramelise & braise
stages.method: Reheat saucepan with 1 tbsp pork fat. Add the sugar and cook for 3–5 mins on medium heat until caramelised. Return pork and fry 2–3 mins until coated. Add shoaxing wine, light soy, dark soy, beer, bay leaves, star anise and remaining spring onions and ginger. Bring to a boil then simmer with lid on for 45 mins–1 hour, stirring occasionally. Consider thickening with cornflour. Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve with steaming rice and blanched veg.

stages.label: Mix dough

ingredients.item: 3/4 cup granulated sugar, plus extra for sprinkling
1 tablespoon fennel seeds, lightly crushed, plus extra for tops of biscotti
1 large egg white, slightly beaten, reserved for egg wash and for dough, if needed
Set oven rack to middle position. Preheat the oven to 300 degrees F. Line a large sheet pan with parchment paper.

In the bowl of a stand mixer, mix together the dry ingredients, lemon zest, and fennel seed with your fingertips, pressing out any lumps of brown sugar. Attach bowl to mixer, and set up the paddle attachment.
Add in whole eggs and yolk, and mix.
Remove the super-sticky dough from the bowl (a little flour will help) and knead a few times on a lightly floured surface. Cut into two even pieces. If you have a kitchen scale, use it.

Form each piece into a log about 12 inches long by 2 inches wide, and transfer to the parchment-lined pan. It might be easier for you to form the logs right on the parchment paper. Keep the logs a good distance apart, at least 4 inches. The logs spread a lot, so don't put them too close to the sides of the pan, either.
Brush some egg wash on each log. Sprinkle tops with extra fennel seeds and sugar. Bake for 50 minutes.
Remove from oven and cool the pan on a rack for 10 minutes. DON'T TURN OFF THE OVEN!
Separate each log from the parchment paper by running a metal spatula underneath.
On a cutting board, using a serrated knife, cut the logs on the diagonal into 1/2-inch slices.
Lay the biscotti cut-side down on a new, unlined sheet pan. Bake for 15 minutes. Remove pan from oven, flip biscotti over, and bake for another 15 minutes.
Remove pan and transfer biscotti to a cooling rack to cool completely. While biscotti are cooling, make a pot of espresso.
Store cooled biscotti in an air-tight container.

stages.method: Set oven rack to middle. Preheat to 150°C/300°F. Line a large sheet pan with parchment. In a stand mixer bowl, combine dry ingredients, lemon zest and fennel seed with fingertips. Attach bowl with paddle. Add whole eggs and yolk and mix. Turn out the sticky dough, knead a few times on a floured surface and cut into two equal pieces.

stages.label: First bake
stages.method: Form each piece into a log about 30cm long by 5cm wide on the parchment-lined pan. Keep logs at least 10cm apart — they spread significantly. Brush with egg wash, sprinkle with extra fennel seeds and sugar. Bake for 50 minutes.

stages.label: Slice & second bake
stages.method: Remove from oven and cool on rack for 10 minutes. Do not turn off the oven. Separate each log from parchment with a metal spatula. On a cutting board, slice logs diagonally into 1cm slices with a serrated knife. Lay cut-side down on an unlined sheet pan. Bake 15 minutes, flip, bake another 15 minutes. Cool completely on a rack.

stages.label: Activate yeast
stages.method: Add yeast and sugar to bowl. Stir in warm milk to dissolve. Cover for 10 minutes until foamy.

stages.label: Make & prove dough
stages.method: Sift flour into a large bowl and add salt. Add the yeast liquid and mix with a spoon. Add olive oil and knead by hand for 2 minutes — dough will be very soft and slightly sticky. Cover and leave in a warm place for 1 hour until doubled in size.

stages.label: Shape & cook
stages.method: Sprinkle work surface with flour and knock back the dough. Divide into 6 equal balls. Cover while you work to prevent drying. Roll each ball into a circle 18cm across, about 5mm thick. Cook in a hot dry pan on medium heat for about 3 minutes per side until golden brown and bubbling. While one is cooking, roll the next.

stages.label: Garnish

ingredients.item: Parsley, Red pepper flakes, etc. for garnish

stages.method: Finely chop parsley. Brush each flatbread with olive oil and sprinkle with parsley and red pepper flakes.

title: No-Bake Mango Lime Cheesecake (1)

stages.label: Crust

ingredients.item: 55 g unsalted butter, melted, plus extra for greasing

stages.method: Generously butter and line an 8–9 inch springform pan with parchment. Grease again and chill. Crush crackers to fine crumbs. Mix with brown sugar and melted butter until it resembles wet sand. Press into an even layer in the pan. Freeze for 10–15 minutes.

stages.label: Filling

ingredients.item: 450 g cream cheese, cubed, at room temperature
1 tsp lime juice and 1 tsp lime zest

stages.method: Hydrate gelatin: sprinkle over 60ml room-temperature water, let swell 5 minutes, then stir in 60ml hot water until dissolved. Meanwhile, whip heavy cream and 100g sugar to stiff peaks. Transfer to a bowl. In the same clean bowl, beat cream cheese and remaining sugar until smooth (5 min). Add mango pulp and lime juice, beat until combined. Mix in dissolved gelatin. Fold in whipped cream, lime zest and cardamom by hand. Pour over crust, smooth top and refrigerate 30 minutes.

stages.label: Glaze & set
stages.method: Hydrate second gelatin packet as before. Whisk mango pulp and sugar, then mix in dissolved gelatin, lime juice and zest. Pour over cheesecake and swirl to coat evenly. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate until fully set, at least 6 hours or overnight.

notes: Avoid using fresh mango pulp here: An enzyme in raw mango can prevent the gelatin from setting. (Canned mangoes don’t have that enzyme; it’s destroyed when they’re heated to high temperatures to be preserved.)

stages.label: Cake
stages.method: Preheat oven to 160°C fan. Line a 2lb loaf tin with parchment. Beat together butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Add self-raising flour and eggs and beat until smooth. Fold through raspberries and white chocolate chips. Pour into tin and bake for 55–65 minutes. Cool in tin 10 minutes then turn onto a wire rack.

stages.label: Buttercream
stages.method: Melt white chocolate and leave to cool. Beat butter in a mixer until really smooth. Add icing sugar and beat until smooth. Add cooled white chocolate and beat again.

stages.label: Decorate
stages.method: Spread or pipe buttercream over cooled cake. Top with fresh raspberries, freeze-dried raspberries and white chocolate chips.

stages.label: Prep pecans

ingredients.item: 100g pecans, toasted and roughly chopped, plus extra to decorate

stages.method: Toast and chop pecans in a pan with 1 tbsp butter, 25g sugar and 1 tsp cinnamon. Set aside.

stages.label: Batter

ingredients.item: 150g butter, melted, plus extra for greasing

stages.method: Preheat oven to 180°C. Grease and line the bases of 2 × 18cm sandwich tins. Whisk melted butter, sugar and eggs in a large bowl until thoroughly combined and almost doubled in volume. Sift together flour, bicarb, salt and spices and fold very gently into the liquid mixture, taking care not to knock out air. Fold in orange zest, sultanas, grated carrot and pecans. Divide between tins. Bake for about 30 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool in tins.

stages.label: Icing & assemble

ingredients.item: Zest of ½ lemon and a squeeze of juice

stages.method: Beat together cream cheese, light brown sugar and lemon zest and juice. Refrigerate until the cakes are cool. Remove cakes from tins. Spread half the icing on one cake, place the other on top, ice the top and decorate with remaining pecans.

stages.label: Crust

ingredients.item: 140g digestive biscuits, made into fine crumbs (add 2 extra biscuits if you like a thicker base)
1 tbsp sugar, granulated or golden caster

stages.method: Bring all ingredients to room temperature 2 hours before cooking. Set oven shelf to middle and preheat to 160°C fan. Line the base of a 23cm springform tin. Melt 85g butter, stir in biscuit crumbs and 1 tbsp sugar. Press into base and bake 10 minutes. Cool on wire rack. Increase oven to 200°C fan.

stages.label: Filling

ingredients.item: 900g Philadelphia cheese, or other full-fat soft cheese
finely grated zest of 1 lemon (about 2 tsp)

stages.method: Beat 900g cream cheese at medium-low speed for 2 minutes. On low, gradually add 250g sugar, 3 tbsp flour and a pinch of salt, scraping down twice. Add 1½ tsp vanilla, 2 tsp lemon zest and 1½ tsp lemon juice. Whisk in 3 eggs and 1 yolk one at a time, scraping twice. Add 200ml soured cream and whisk to blend — batter should be smooth and airy. Brush tin sides with melted butter. Pour in filling. Bake 10 minutes at 200°C.

stages.label: Slow bake & cool
stages.method: Reduce oven to 90°C fan and bake 45 minutes more — filling should have a slight wobble. Turn off oven. For a creamy centre open the door slightly; for a drier texture leave closed. Cool in oven for 2 hours. The top may crack slightly.

stages.label: Topping & set
stages.method: Combine reserved soured cream with 142ml carton soured cream, 1 tbsp sugar and 2 tsp lemon juice. Spread over cheesecake to the edges. Cover loosely with foil and refrigerate at least 8 hours or overnight.

stages.label: Dough & first prove

ingredients.item: 4 ounces of butter, room temperature (113 g)

stages.method: In a stand mixer combine 92g sugar, ½ tsp salt and 85g soft butter. Beat at medium-high for 1 minute until fluffy. Scrape down, add 1 egg and lemon zest; mix 2 minutes. Add 454g bread flour, 7g instant yeast and 236ml buttermilk. Mix with paddle on medium-low until just combined, then switch to dough hook and knead 10 minutes on medium. Place in an oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap and leave to rise for 2 hours.

stages.label: Fill, roll & shape
stages.method: Oil work surface and rolling pin. Knead dough briefly to release air, then roll to approx 35 × 45cm. Mix ½ cup white sugar, 1½ tbsp cinnamon and ¼ cup brown sugar; sprinkle generously over dough. Roll up tightly. Trim 2.5cm from each end, then slice into 12 equal rolls.

stages.label: Second prove & bake
stages.method: Oil and line a large baking dish with parchment. Place rolls with ~1cm space between them. Cover and prove 90 minutes. Begin preheating oven to 175°C at 60 minutes. Brush tops with melted butter. Bake 20 minutes until golden brown. Cool 5 minutes.

stages.label: Frosting

ingredients.item: 8 ounces of cream cheese, room temperature (227 g)
Heavy cream (if needed to thin out frosting)

stages.method: Beat 227g cream cheese and 113g butter until smooth. Add 256g sifted powdered sugar and beat again. Thin with a little heavy cream if needed. Spread generously over warm rolls. Remove rolls as a unit to a wire rack.

stages.label: Soak dal
stages.method: Rinse lentils until water runs clear. Drain and place in a deep pan with generously boiled water. Soak for at least 6 hours or up to 24 hours.

stages.label: Boil dal
stages.method: Rinse soaked lentils and return to a large pan. Cover with cold water, bring to boil and continue boiling for 45 minutes. Skim off any scum. Dal should be soft enough to crush against the side of the pan.

stages.label: Onion & tomato base

ingredients.item: chilli powder ¾ tsp (or to taste)

stages.method: While dal boils, melt 60g butter in a frying pan over medium heat. Add onions and cook 15 minutes. Add ginger and garlic and fry 5 more minutes. Add tomato purée, salt and chilli powder, stir well and take off the heat.

stages.label: Long simmer
stages.method: Once dal is cooked, keep just enough water to cover and drain the rest. Add the onion-tomato mixture and the milk. Bring to boil then simmer, stirring every now and then, for around 1½ hours. If sauce runs low, top up with equal parts whole milk and water. The sauce should turn darker, richer and creamier — keep going beyond 1½ hours if not yet rich and dark. Taste, adjust salt and chilli, then stir in the remaining 30g butter just before serving.

stages.label: Aromatics

ingredients.item: 1 tsp olive oil or coconut oil
1 tbsp fresh ginger peeled and grated
½ finely chopped red chilli (or ½ tsp dried chilli flakes)

stages.method: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add onion and cook gently for 5 minutes. Add garlic, ginger and red chilli and cook a few more minutes.

stages.label: Spices
stages.method: Add all the spices to the pan and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.

stages.label: Simmer

ingredients.item: 1 cup (200g) dried red lentils, uncooked (rinsed and drained)

stages.method: Add lentils, tinned tomatoes with their juice, coconut milk and stock. Stir to combine. Season with salt and pepper. Cook on medium-low heat for 15–20 minutes until reduced and thick. Taste and add more chilli if desired. Can leave on very low heat a little longer.

stages.label: Finish
stages.method: Stir in lemon juice and spinach until wilted. Serve warm with rice, naan or poppadoms.

stages.label: Crust
stages.method: Preheat oven to 180°C (non-fan). Crush crackers to very fine uniform crumbs. Mix with sugar, egg yolk, salt and melted butter until it resembles wet sand. Press firmly up the sides and across the bottom of a 23cm pie plate using a straight-sided glass. Place on a sheet pan and bake until fragrant and dark brown at the edges, 8–12 minutes. Reduce oven to 160°C.

stages.label: Filling
stages.method: Whisk lime zest, egg yolks, condensed milk, lime juice, salt and vanilla until completely smooth. Pour into the pie plate and smooth the surface. Bake until set with just a slight wobble in the centre, 15–20 minutes. Cool completely at room temperature (about 1 hour) then refrigerate until cold, at least 2 hours.

stages.label: Topping
stages.method: Work cream cheese and powdered sugar together with a spatula until smooth. Switch to a whisk and slowly drizzle in cold cream, whisking constantly to prevent lumps, until you have a softly whipped cream forming medium peaks. Spread over the cold pie nearly to the edges. Chill until set, at least 15 minutes.

stages.label: Marinate chicken tikka

ingredients.item: skinless chicken thighs or breasts 675g, (2½cm chunks)
root ginger 1 tbsp, peeled, finely grated
garlic 2 cloves, finely grated or crushed
root ginger 1 tbsp, peeled, finely grated
salt ¼ tsp, or to taste

stages.method: Place chicken in a non-reactive bowl. Rub in salt and lemon juice. Prod lightly with the tip of a knife and rub in again. Set aside 20 minutes. Add ginger, garlic, cumin, paprika, chilli powder, cream and garam masala. Mix well, cover and refrigerate for 6–8 hours.

stages.label: Masala sauce

ingredients.item: 1 Tin peeled plum or 2 whole medium tomatoes, peeled and very finely chopped

stages.method: Heat 4 tbsp oil in a large lidded pan over medium-high heat. Add onions and fry until brown, 6–7 minutes. Add ginger and garlic and fry 1 minute. Add ground coriander, turmeric, chilli powder and paprika. Stir 10 seconds, then add yogurt 1 tbsp at a time, stirring and frying until each spoonful is absorbed. Add tomatoes and fry 3–4 minutes until pulpy. Add stock and salt, bring to a simmer, cover and simmer gently 15–20 minutes until thick. Stir in garam masala and coriander.

stages.label: Grill tikka
stages.method: Preheat grill to highest setting. Thread chicken onto skewers (flat sword-like ones are best). Brush with 3 tbsp oil and balance skewers on the rim of a shallow baking tray so meat is suspended. Grill about 13cm from heat for 6 minutes each side until lightly browned, cooked through and charred in places. Cut a large piece to the centre to check no trace of pink.

stages.label: Combine & serve
stages.method: Reheat the masala sauce. Fold in the grilled chicken pieces. Serve immediately.

stages.label: Stage 1
stages.method: test
